title: Jibri
description: Jitsi Broadcasting Infrastructure - a recording and live-streaming component that captures Jitsi Meet conferences via headless Chrome and ffmpeg.
installation: Jibri (Jitsi Broadcasting Infrastructure) is NOT a standalone app: it is the recording/live-streaming worker of the Jitsi Meet stack. It runs a headless Chrome + ffmpeg, joins a conference as a hidden participant, and records to a file (or streams to RTMP/YouTube). It keeps NO SQL database - all state is file-based and conference/auth state lives in Jitsi's Prosody XMPP server - which is why this template declares NO add-on; recordings are written to the mounted /config volume (JIBRI_RECORDING_DIR=/config/recordings). Jibri exposes only an INTERNAL control/health HTTP API on port 2222 (http://host:2222/jibri/api/v1.0/health); it has no end-user web UI, so do not expect a browser page. REQUIRED COMPANION SERVICES (deploy these as SEPARATE Kubero apps - see the 'jitsi' template; ALL must run the SAME Jitsi image version): (a) jitsi/prosody - XMPP signaling server Jibri logs into; (b) jitsi/jicofo - conference focus that dispatches recording requests to Jibri; (c) jitsi/jvb - the videobridge routing media; (d) jitsi/web - the Meet frontend. Jibri authenticates to Prosody with TWO accounts that MUST match the credentials configured on the Jitsi side: the RECORDER client account (JIBRI_RECORDER_USER/JIBRI_RECORDER_PASSWORD on XMPP_RECORDER_DOMAIN) and the internal MUC control account (JIBRI_XMPP_USER/JIBRI_XMPP_PASSWORD on XMPP_AUTH_DOMAIN). FIRST-RUN STEPS: 1) Rotate JIBRI_RECORDER_PASSWORD and JIBRI_XMPP_PASSWORD (placeholder 'replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-16' values) and register those same accounts on the Prosody pod (prosodyctl register / the docker-jitsi-meet jibri provisioning), and set ENABLE_RECORDING=1 + JIBRI_* on the jitsi web/jicofo pods so they advertise recording. 2) Point XMPP_SERVER at your Prosody service (default 'jitsi-prosody', matching the jitsi template). 3) Mount the SAME /config used by the rest of the stack if you want finalize scripts/recordings shared. KERNEL/HOST CAVEAT: Jibri needs the host 'snd-aloop' ALSA loopback kernel module loaded (modprobe snd-aloop) and access to /dev/snd for audio capture, plus extra capabilities for headless Chrome - a vanilla pod cannot record without the node providing snd-aloop and the pod running privileged or with the audio device mounted; provision that on the node before relying on recording.
